what_it_does: This file is a design-system audit report that catalogs hard-coded size and color literals that should be replaced by tokens, candidate unmapped color tokens, scenario coverage gaps, orphaned addresses, and CSS/data-ui hygiene issues across a set of HTML mockup screens.
observations: 31 literals are flagged as should_use_var, mostly size tokens (5px, 8px, 12px, 4px, 16px, etc.) and a smaller set of color tokens.
The most frequent literal is 5px (340 occurrences), mapped to the token r-sm, and it appears in all 20 listed screens.
27 candidate new colour tokens are listed without matching tokens, many concentrated in A2-rhm-mobile-elevated.html, A3-settings-elevated.html, and SCENARIO-PLAYER.html.
Coverage gaps include six prefixed identifiers: ENG-branch, ENG-branches-pause, ENG-volatile, FRM-divergence, INB-target, and WALK-session.
Address orphans include 2 unregistered run:// addresses and 45 unused ui:// addresses.
Three screens are flagged as missing both a CSS link and any data-ui reference: A2-rhm-mobile-elevated.html, A3-settings-elevated.html, and SCENARIO-PLAYER.html.
The summary object aggregates counts for screens, literals, candidates, gaps, orphans, and hygiene issues.
standouts: The same three screens (A2-rhm-mobile-elevated, A3-settings-elevated, SCENARIO-PLAYER) account for most unmapped colors and all hygiene misses.
Radius literals 5px/8px/12px are pervasive across every screen, suggesting a near-universal tokenization opportunity.
The unused address list (45 entries) is far larger than the unregistered address list (2 entries).
conventions: Uses a regular JSON schema with arrays of objects containing literal, count, screens, matches_token, and kind.
Token names follow a semantic shorthand: r-* for radius, sp-* for spacing, fs-* for font size, and descriptive names for colors.
Coverage gaps use prefixed categorical identifiers (ENG-, FRM-, INB-, WALK-).
Addresses use URI-like schemes: run:// for runtime/demo paths and ui:// for interface action paths.
concerns: Candidate new colour tokens include many near-black/near-gray variants that may be duplicates or theme-specific, but no token names or grouping are provided.
The 45 unused ui:// addresses could indicate stale registry entries, incomplete wiring, or reserved names; the report does not distinguish among them.
The three screens missing CSS and data-ui references may be standalone previews rather than integrated mockups, yet they are treated uniformly in the audit.
Coverage gaps are listed only as identifiers, with no associated screens, counts, or descriptions, making prioritization unclear.
notes: A design-system consistency report that flags tokenizable literals, orphaned addresses, and coverage gaps across 20 HTML mockups, with a few screens carrying most of the unmapped color and hygiene debt.
questions: What exact rule distinguishes a literal flagged as should_use_var from one listed as candidate_new_colour_token?
Are coverage_gaps missing screens, missing address mappings, or missing test scenarios?
Is the large unused address inventory expected (reserved) or indicative of dead registry entries?
Why do A2-rhm-mobile-elevated, A3-settings-elevated, and SCENARIO-PLAYER share so many unmapped colors and hygiene misses?
Are the candidate colors intended for a new theme or an elevated/dark mode?
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to create a feedback loop between static HTML mockups and a token/address registry, so maintainers can find where values and references have drifted from the shared system.
novelty: It combines a visual-token audit (literal-to-token matching) with address-space hygiene checks and scenario coverage gaps in one report, treating design artifacts as nodes in a code-like dependency graph.
contribution: Provides a normalized, machine-readable ledger of design-system drift that a fused system could use to prioritize tokenization, identify orphaned or missing address registrations, and locate screens not wired to shared CSS or data-ui references.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON audit report for a design system that lists hard-coded size and color literals that should use tokens, candidate unmapped colors, scenario coverage gaps, orphaned run:// and ui:// addresses, and CSS/data-ui hygiene issues across 20 HTML mockup screens.
intention_for_embedding: To surface where mockups deviate from the token and address registry so maintainers can align values, fill coverage gaps, and clean up unused or unregistered references.
